```{r pathway-enrichment-fisher}
  run_fisher(hc_proteins, erk_neg,  bg_genes, "ERK negative markers"),
  run_fisher(hc_proteins, erk_all,  bg_genes, "ERK all markers"),
  run_fisher(hc_proteins, top_genes, bg_genes, "5'TOP genes")
)

fisher_results %>%
  mutate(significant = if_else(p_value < 0.05, "✓", "")) %>%
  kable(digits = c(0, 0, 0, 2, 4, 0),
        col.names = c("Pathway", "In HC regs", "In background only",
                       "Odds ratio", "p (Fisher)", "Sig."),
        caption = "Enrichment of pathway markers among HC regulators") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```


```{r pathway-enrichment-bar, fig.height=5}
fisher_results %>%
  mutate(nlp = -log10(p_value),
         pathway = factor(pathway, levels = rev(pathway))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = nlp, y = pathway, fill = nlp > -log10(0.05))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", colour = "red3") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "grey70"),
                    guide = "none") +
  labs(x = expression(-log[10](p)), y = NULL,
       title = "Pathway Marker Enrichment Among HC Regulators",
       subtitle = "Dashed line = p = 0.05") +
  theme_report()
```

### Sign Concordance: Do mTOR/ERK Markers Match Expected Direction?

> **What this answers:** mTOR-positive markers (expression tracks pathway
> activity) should appear as **positive** HC regulators if they genuinely
> drive translational upregulation. mTOR-negative markers should appear as
> **negative** regulators. If the signs are concordant, the model is
> capturing biologically meaningful directionality. Discordance would suggest
> the model is picking up indirect or confounded associations.

```{r sign-concordance}
hc_with_sign <- hc_A %>% dplyr::select(protein, dom)

sign_check <- function(pathway_genes, pathway_label, expected_sign) {
  hits <- hc_with_sign %>% filter(protein %in% pathway_genes)
  if (nrow(hits) == 0) return(tibble(pathway = pathway_label,
                                      n_hits = 0, concordant = NA, pct = NA))
  tibble(
    pathway    = pathway_label,
    n_hits     = nrow(hits),
    concordant = sum(hits$dom == expected_sign),
    pct        = sum(hits$dom == expected_sign) / nrow(hits) * 100
  )
}

sign_tbl <- bind_rows(
  sign_check(mtor_pos, "mTOR positive → expect positive HC", "positive"),
  sign_check(mtor_neg, "mTOR negative → expect negative HC", "negative"),
  sign_check(erk_pos,  "ERK positive → expect positive HC",  "positive"),
  sign_check(erk_neg,  "ERK negative → expect negative HC",  "negative")
)

sign_tbl %>%
  kable(digits = 1,
        col.names = c("Pathway / expected sign", "N in HC", "Concordant", "% concordant"),
        caption = "Sign concordance: do pathway markers match expected regulator direction?") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### Leakage Check: Are mTOR/ERK Markers in "A only" or "Both A & B"?

> **What this answers:** This is the mechanistic crux. If mTOR-positive markers
> appear in "A only" (protein predicts residuals but mRNA doesn't), they are
> acting post-transcriptionally — consistent with mTOR's translational arm
> (4E-BP1/S6K1). If they appear in "Both A & B", the signal may be mTOR's
> transcriptional arm (PGC-1α → mitochondrial gene expression) leaking
> through the OLS correction. This distinction directly addresses the concern
> about mitochondrial protein regulators.


```{r leakage-pathway, fig.height=6}
leakage_class <- tibble(
  gene = c(only_A, in_both, only_B),
  overlap_cat = c(rep("A only", length(only_A)),
                  rep("Both A & B", length(in_both)),
                  rep("B only", length(only_B)))
)

pathway_in_overlap <- leakage_class %>%
  mutate(
    is_mtor_pos = gene %in% mtor_pos,
    is_mtor_neg = gene %in% mtor_neg,
    is_erk_pos  = gene %in% erk_pos,
    is_erk_neg  = gene %in% erk_neg,
    is_5top     = gene %in% top_genes
  )

# ── Summary table ──
leakage_summary <- pathway_in_overlap %>%
  group_by(overlap_cat) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total    = n(),
    mTOR_pos   = sum(is_mtor_pos),
    mTOR_neg   = sum(is_mtor_neg),
    ERK_pos    = sum(is_erk_pos),
    ERK_neg    = sum(is_erk_neg),
    `5TOP`     = sum(is_5top),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

leakage_summary %>%
  kable(caption = "Pathway markers by overlap category (A only / Both / B only)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)

# ── Visual ──
leakage_long <- pathway_in_overlap %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("is_"),
               names_to = "pathway", values_to = "member") %>%
  filter(member) %>%
  mutate(pathway = recode(pathway,
                          is_mtor_pos = "mTOR+", is_mtor_neg = "mTOR−",
                          is_erk_pos = "ERK+", is_erk_neg = "ERK−",
                          is_5top = "5'TOP"))

if (nrow(leakage_long) > 0) {
  leakage_long %>%
    dplyr::count(overlap_cat, pathway, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(overlap_cat = factor(overlap_cat,
                                levels = c("A only", "Both A & B", "B only"))) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = pathway, y = n, fill = overlap_cat)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("A only" = "#E64B35",
                                  "Both A & B" = "#91D1C2",
                                  "B only" = "#4DBBD5")) +
    labs(x = "Pathway marker set", y = "# HC regulators", fill = "Overlap category",
         title = "Where Do Pathway Markers Land?",
         subtitle = "A only = post-transcriptional; Both = co-transcriptional leakage?") +
    theme_report()
} else {
  cat("No pathway markers found among HC regulators.\n")
}
```

### 5'TOP Genes as Targets of mTOR-Positive Regulators

> **What this answers:** The most direct mechanistic prediction: mTOR-positive
> HC regulators (proteins that track mTOR activity) should specifically predict
> 5'TOP gene residuals, because mTOR drives 5'TOP translation via 4E-BP1.
> We test whether the median LASSO coefficient of mTOR-positive regulators
> is higher for 5'TOP targets than for non-5'TOP targets.

```{r 5top-mtor-link, fig.height=5}
# ── Get all Model A coefficients involving mTOR-positive regulators ──
mtor_pos_in_hc <- intersect(mtor_pos, hc_A$protein)

if (length(mtor_pos_in_hc) > 0) {
  mtor_coefs <- coef_A_df %>%
    filter(regulator %in% mtor_pos_in_hc) %>%
    mutate(target_is_5top = target %in% top_genes,
           target_group = if_else(target_is_5top, "5'TOP target", "Other target"))

  if (nrow(mtor_coefs) > 0) {
    # ── Boxplot of absolute beta ──
    ggplot(mtor_coefs, aes(x = target_group, y = abs(beta), fill = target_group)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("5'TOP target" = "#E64B35", "Other target" = "grey70")) +
      labs(x = NULL, y = "|β| (LASSO coefficient)",
           title = "mTOR-Positive Regulators: Effect Size on 5'TOP vs Other Targets",
           subtitle = sprintf("Regulators tested: %s",
                              paste(mtor_pos_in_hc, collapse = ", "))) +
      theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "none")

    wt2 <- wilcox.test(abs(beta) ~ target_is_5top, data = mtor_coefs)
    cat(sprintf("Wilcoxon p-value (|β| for 5'TOP vs other): %.2e\n", wt2$p.value))
    cat(sprintf("Median |β| — 5'TOP targets: %.4f, Other: %.4f\n",
                median(abs(mtor_coefs$beta[mtor_coefs$target_is_5top])),
                median(abs(mtor_coefs$beta[!mtor_coefs$target_is_5top]))))
  } else {
    cat("No coefficients found for mTOR-positive HC regulators.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("No mTOR-positive markers among HC regulators — cannot test 5'TOP link.\n")
}
```

# Summary {.tabset}

> **Aim:** Compile all key metrics into a single reference table for quick
> consultation. This table should contain enough information to write the
> results paragraph of a paper.

## Key Metrics

```{r summary-table}
tribble(
  ~Metric,                                     ~Value,
  "Training dataset",                          "Satpathy 2021 LSCC tumour",
  "Filtered genes × samples",                 sprintf("%d × %d", n_genes, n_samples),
  "Median per-gene mRNA–protein ρ",            sprintf("%.3f", med_gene_cor),
  "Median per-sample mRNA–protein ρ",          sprintf("%.3f", med_cor),
  "Outlier samples flagged",                   as.character(nrow(outlier_samples)),
  "Median OLS R² (protein ~ own mRNA)",        sprintf("%.3f", med_ols_r2),
  "HC regulators (Model A — protein)",         as.character(nrow(hc_A)),
  "  — positive",                              as.character(sum(hc_A$dom == "positive")),
  "  — negative",                              as.character(sum(hc_A$dom == "negative")),
  "  — post-transcriptional (A only)",         as.character(length(only_A)),
  "  — co-transcriptional leakage (A∩B)",      as.character(length(in_both)),
  "HC regulators (Model B — mRNA)",            as.character(nrow(hc_B)),
  "Median R² — Model A (CV)",                  sprintf("%.3f", median(r2_A, na.rm = TRUE)),
  "Median R² — Model B (CV)",                  sprintf("%.3f", median(r2_B, na.rm = TRUE)),
  "Median R² — Model C (CV)",                  sprintf("%.3f", median(r2_C, na.rm = TRUE)),
  "% targets A > B",                           sprintf("%.1f%%", pct_a_wins),
  "LASSO vs Elastic Net ρ",                    sprintf("%.3f", cor_val),
  "External datasets evaluated",               as.character(nrow(ext_qc_tbl))
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Key analysis metrics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
